steps.title: Trace the poem's parallel structure
steps.content:
The poem draws an explicit parallel: a flower flourishes when "the dew and the rain draw it forth" and languishes without them; the mind flourishes when "fed with the sweet dew of the fostering spirit" and begins "straightway to languish" without it. Both the flower and the mind are shown thriving only when properly fed, and both wilt without that nourishment.
  • Choice A — 'It becomes increasingly vigorous with the passage of time.': The poem never mentions time as a factor; vigor depends on nourishment, not duration. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It draws strength from changes in the weather.': The rain and dew represent needed water, not changing weather conditions. The poem does not say weather variation strengthens either the mind or flower. ✗
  • Choice C — 'It requires proper nourishment in order to thrive.': Matches the poem's central parallel exactly — both the flower and the mind need feeding to flourish and languish without it. ✓
  • Choice D — 'It perseveres despite challenging circumstances.': The opposite of what the poem shows; both the mind and flower languish right away when nourishment is absent, not persevere. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the match
steps.content:
The poem's single sustained point is that mind and flower share a dependence on nourishment to thrive. Choice C is the only answer capturing this.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
